name: Paraguay (reverse)

colors.symbolism: justice, equality and the bravery of the Paraguayan patriots
peace, unity and the purity of the patriotic cause

description: The reverse of the Paraguayan flag. Three equal horizontal stripes — red, white, blue — with the Treasury seal (Sello de la Hacienda) centred on the white stripe: a yellow lion guarding a Phrygian cap on a pole, with the motto 'PAZ Y JUSTICIA' ('Peace and Justice') above, all encircled by a green palm-and-olive wreath.
history: Paraguay is unusual among national flags in that the obverse and reverse differ. Both sides carry the same red-white-blue tricolour, but the obverse bears the national coat of arms (Escudo de la República, Star of May) and the reverse bears the Treasury seal (Sello de la Hacienda), depicting a lion before a pole bearing a Phrygian cap (gorro frigio), an iconography drawn from the French Revolution via the Argentine independence movement. The dual-side design was decreed on 25 November 1842.

features.symbolism: the tricolour is traditionally said to recall the colours of the French Revolution

a yellow lion guarding a pole bearing a red Phrygian cap (gorro frigio), surrounded by the motto 'PAZ Y JUSTICIA' and a green palm-and-olive wreath; the Phrygian cap is a Revolution-era symbol of liberty inherited via the Argentine independence iconography
